channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: First-term Michigan senator assails Trump's policies in Democratic response
item.description: LANSING, Mich. (AP) — The Democratic response to President Donald Trump’sjoint congressional address Tuesday was delivered by a first-term Michigan senator who built an extensive national security background before entering politics less than a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: One moment, calm waters. The next, a 900-pound dolphin landed on their boat
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — A placid fishing trip turned “berserk” for three New Zealand men when a dolphin weighing more than 400 kilograms (900 pounds) appeared to fall out of the sky before crash-landing in their small boat.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Lutnick: Trump may announce tariff compromise with Canada, Mexico on Wednesday
item.description: Trump implementing 25 percent tariffs triggered retaliatory measures from both nations, with major corporations also warning that it could lead to potential price increases for consumers.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Former Los Angeles fire chief fails to get job back after ouster following historic deadly wildfire
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The former Los Angeles fire chief who was ousted by Mayor Karen Bass after the most destructive wildfire in city history failed to win back her job Tuesday, falling short with her argument that her dismissal was based on false accusations about her conduct and decision-making.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. BLOOD, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Why Trump's speech to Congress isn't a 'State of the Union' address
item.description: It looks like the State of the Union, and will be carried on live television, just like those annual addresses are. But it's called something else: a joint address to Congress. And it has its origins in the first term of President Ronald Reagan.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Man sentenced to 4 years in prison for Culver City hate crime assault
item.description: A previously homeless man has been sentenced to more than four years in prison for an unprovoked racially motivated assault on an Asian American woman in Culver City. Jesse Allen Lindsey, 38, pleaded guilty in December to a federal hate crime charge for the 2021 attack. Around 1 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Idaho murders case: Bryan Kohberger judge ends 'pervasive' secret filings restricting what public can see
item.description: Judge overseeing Bryan Kohberger's Idaho student murders case says practice of filing many documents under seal violates the First Amendment and will end.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine selling T-shirts with Zelensky quote from Trump-Vance meeting
item.description: The Ukrainian government has launched a T-shirt fundraising effort seeking to capitalize on Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky's now-infamous Oval Office meeting on Friday with President Trump and Vice President Vance.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Census Bureau under Trump seeks permission to delete questions about gender identity
item.description: The U.S. Census Bureau under the Trump administration has sought permission to delete questions about gender identity from a monthly survey that gathers near real-time data about American life.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jimmy John's is giving out free sandwiches. Here's how to redeem the limited-time deal
item.description: Sandwich chain Jimmy John’s is giving away $1 million worth of toasted sandwiches on Tuesday to celebrate a new product launch. The chain said it is offering this free food giveaway to show appreciation for customers’ patience as it perfected the new product.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Here are the White House guests for Trump's address
item.description: Fifteen individuals will sit with the first lady, with the invited individuals intended to highlight Trump's accomplishments.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Here are the White House guests for Trump's address
item.description: The White House on Tuesday unveiled the list of guests who will sit with first lady Melania Trump at the Capitol during President Donald Trump’s address to a joint session of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Target CEO: Customers will 'likely see price increases' after Trump tariffs
item.description: The tariffs imposed by President Donald Trump on Tuesday are likely to have a direct impact on the wallets of Americans, a major CEO said. In an appearance on CNBC on Tuesday, Target CEO Brian Cornell said the company will probably raise prices on produce and fruits as early as this week.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Wall Street falls again as losses wipe out all post-election gains for the S&P 500
item.description: Stocks tumbled on Wall Street Tuesday as a trade war between the U.S. and its key trading partners escalated and threatened to wipe out all the gains for the S&P 500 since Election Day.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Pope stable, but will sleep with ventilation mask, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis was in stable condition Tuesday and breathing with just the help of supplemental oxygen after respiratory crises a day earlier, but will resume using a ventilation mask at night, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Powerful US storms kill 2, bring threats from critical fire weather to blizzard conditions
item.description: Powerful storms have killed two people in Mississippi, torn the roofs off an apartment building and a nursing home in Oklahoma and threatened communities across the nation.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Martin And Jack Brook, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump promises to keep up 'swift and unrelenting action' in speech to Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump vowed to keep up his campaign of “swift and unrelenting action” in reorienting the nation’s economy, immigration and foreign policy as he delivered an unyielding address Tuesday night to Congress and the nation.
item.dc:creator: Zeke Miller, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: How South Carolina's execution of a condemned killer by firing squad will unfold
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — When a South Carolina man who killed his ex-girlfriend’s parents with a baseball bat steps into the death row chamber Friday night, it won’t be lethal injection or electrocution that ends his life.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pope Francis suffers new breathing crises, is back on noninvasive ventilation, Vatican says
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis suffered two new acute respiratory crises Monday and was put back on noninvasive mechanical ventilation, in another setback to his battle to fight pneumonia, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Former NYC daycare owner learns fate following toddler's fentanyl poisoning death
item.description: A former daycare owner in the Bronx has been sentenced to 45 years after pleading guilty to several drug charges that led to the death of a 22-month-old in September 2023.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: GOP bill to ban trans athletes from women’s sports blocked in Senate
item.description: Legislation to prevent transgender athletes from participating in girls’ and women’s sports failed to advance in the Senate on Monday after all Democrats voted against it. The Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act failed to clear an initial procedural hurdle on a 51-45 vote.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, WGN 9

item.title: Trump pauses aid to Ukraine amid clash with Zelensky
item.description: President Trump has ordered a pause on U.S. aid going to Ukraine in its war against Russia, a consequential move as he clashes with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky about the path to ending the conflict. "The President has been clear that he is focused on peace.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Man pleads guilty to murder and attempted murder in shooting at suburban Chicago July 4 parade
item.description: WAUKEGAN, Ill. (AP) — An Illinois man pleaded guilty Monday to killing seven people and injuring dozens more when he opened fire on a 2022 Independence Day parade in a Chicago suburb, a stunning development moments before opening statements in his trial on murder and attempted murder charges.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: GOP rep introduces measure putting Trump face on $100 bill
item.description: First-term GOP Rep. Brandon Gill (Texas) has introduced a measure that would require all $100 bills to have President Trump’s face on them.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KTLA 5

item.title: Michigan mom seeks 'respect' for her 3 missing boys, asks a judge to declare them dead
item.description: ADRIAN, Mich. (AP) — The mother of three missing Michigan boys asked a judge Monday to declare them dead nearly 15 years later, saying they deserve the respect that they didn't get at the end of their young lives.
item.dc:creator: ED WHITE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 2 leaders of a group suspected of smuggling 20,000 immigrants are arrested in Los Angeles
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Federal authorities in Los Angeles arrested two alleged leaders of a criminal organization suspected of smuggling 20,000 people from Guatemala to the U.S. and charging each person as much as $18,000 to get them into the country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: College takes disciplinary action months after anti-Israel agitators seized Columbia University building
item.description: Barnard College took action against several students who were involved in an anti-Israel occupation of a Columbia University building last spring at the New York City campus.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How did Americans view the Trump-Zelenskyy meeting? Poll shows stark divide
item.description: About half of those polled said neither Ukraine nor the U.S. walked away as a winner from last week's meeting.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, KRON4

item.title: US stocks tumble after Trump announces Canadian and Mexican tariffs
item.description: U.S. stocks are falling sharply Monday after President Donald Trump said tariffs he announced on Canada and Mexico will take effect on Tuesday. The bond market also took a hit following the latest warning signal on the strength of the U.S. economy.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Cat food recalled over risk of bird flu weeks after pets euthanized
item.description: The Wild Coast LLC product 16oz and 24oz frozen Boneless Free Range Chicken Formula raw pet food for cats was distributed to pet food retailers in Washington State and Oregon. The product is frozen in a small round white plastic container with a green label.
item.dc:creator: Erin Yudt, KRON4

item.title: Illinois couple's home explodes on wedding day; authorities say suspect was a guest's boyfriend
item.description: Authorities have identified the individual responsible for an explosion in Cicero that happened on Feb. 15 and left one person dead and 11 families displaced, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, KRON4

item.title: Ariana Grande goes full Glinda, 'Wicked' bestie Erivo does Elphaba proud in green on Oscars carpet
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Ariana Grande did her Glinda proud in a pale pink stunner, and her “Wicked” bestie Cynthia Erivo honored Elphaba in deep forest green on the Oscars carpet as high glam took over Sunday. Others went for bold red, classic black and pops of pink.
item.dc:creator: LEANNE ITALIE and BEATRICE DUPUY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Chipotle will absorb tariff-related cost increases, CEO says
item.description: With the Trump administration's 25% tariff on all Mexican imports set to take effect Tuesday, Chipotle has pledged to absorb potential cost increases.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, Fox 5

item.title: Trump announces US crypto reserve
item.description: His announcement comes just after the White House announced it would be welcoming cryptocurrency industry professionals on March 7 in a first-of-its-kind summit.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: Topsy-turvy Oscar season concludes today: Here’s what to look for at the show
item.description: Sunday’s Academy Awards will bring to a close one of the most unpredictable Oscar races in recent memory.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Firefighters working to contain wildfires in North, South Carolina
item.description: Crews have been battling wildfires in North and South Carolina this weekend, competing with dry conditions and gusty winds.
item.dc:creator: Adam Benson, Fox 5
